topicmeta.shortdesc: The NextGen Trading Card Game features exclusive collectible cards inspired by the Rocket Software NextGen Academy and its Rocketeers. The platform empowers users to buy and sell new cards and related products, creating a dynamic marketplace for fans and collectors.

Structural and logical architecture for the ECOMAPP NXTCG database on Db2 13 for z/OS. The database design and architecture includes SKU composition, multiplier-based pricing, and normalized entities with referential integrity.
ECOMAPP database is hosted on Db2 for z/OS platform. Installing ECOMAPP requires you to have a Db2 license called Db2 Connect in order for you to make a connection from the NXTCG backend application.
There are common SQL and API error codes when configuring the ECOMAPP database. Solutions are provided for each issue.

concept.domains: (topic concept) (topic abbrev-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (topic equation-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic mathml-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic svg-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic markup-d xml-d)
concept.title: Database Administrator Guide
concept.shortdesc: The NextGen Trading Card Game features exclusive collectible cards inspired by the Rocket Software NextGen Academy and its Rocketeers. The platform empowers users to buy and sell new cards and related products, creating a dynamic marketplace for fans and collectors.

conbody.p: ECOMAPP is a Db2 13 for z/OS database for NXTCG. It is compromised of 15 normalized tables with full referential integrity and indexed access paths. Administration is driven by source-controlled DDL and JCL pipelines for build, initialization, statistics, grants, and teardown.

ul.li: Complete SKU-based product catalog (4,824 trading card variants)
16 normalized tables with full referential integrity
Performance indexes on all critical access paths
Split initialization files for mainframe line length compliance
Automated JCL for all database operations

conbody.note: NXTCG is planning to extend their future card catalog to NextGen Academy's world-wide.

ul.li: Tablespaces: 15 tablespaces, one per table for flexibility.
Tables: USERS, TOKENS, SERIES, STYLES, STYLE_MODIFIERS, PRODUCT_TYPES, PRODUCT_VARIANTS, VARIANT_IMAGES, INVENTORY, CARTS, CART_ITEMS, ADDRESSES, ORDERS, ORDER_ADDRESSES, ORDER_ITEMS
Indexes: Primary key indexes + business key indexes

conbody.codeblock: <HLQ>.ECOMAPP ├── ALLOC │ └── ECOMALOC │ ├── DDL │ ├── E01STGDB │ ├── E02TBSPC │ ├── E03TABLE │ ├── E04INDEX │ ├── E05CMT │ ├── E06GRANT │ ├── E07INIT1 │ ├── E07INIT2 │ ├── E07INIT3 │ ├── E08INVEN │ ├── E09RPTO1 │ ├── E09RPTO2 │ └── E09RPTO3 │ ├── JCL │ ├── ECOMDDL │ ├── ECOMDROP │ ├── ECOMFILL │ ├── ECOMGRNT │ ├── ECOMINIT │ ├── ECOMRPTS │ └── ECOMRS │ └── REPORTS ├── RPTO1 ├── RPTO2 └── RPTO3

section.title: Db2 Common Codes
section.p: RC=0: Complete success - all DDL executed successfully
RC=4: Warning - generally safe to continue
RC=8: SQL error - review SYSTSPRT
RC=12+: Severe error - STOP and investigate

topic.domains: (topic abbrev-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (topic equation-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic mathml-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic svg-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic markup-d xml-d)
topic.title: ECOMAPP Database Architecture
topic.shortdesc: Structural and logical architecture for the ECOMAPP NXTCG database on Db2 13 for z/OS. The database design and architecture includes SKU composition, multiplier-based pricing, and normalized entities with referential integrity.

body.p: ECOMAPP is a normalized, SKU-driven data model with centralized pricing logic and strong referential integrity. The catalog supports 4,824 sellable SKUs derived from 104 base product types and 63 style modifiers, designed to be scalable and consistent with updates.

section.title: Normalization
section.p: ECOMAPP NXTCG demonstrates a strong level of normalization, with the schema largely conforming to Third Normal Form (3NF). All relations satisfy the requirements of First and Second Normal Forms, and the use of composite‑key tables ensures that each non‑key attribute is fully dependent on the entire primary key.

section.title: SKU Architecture
section.p: ECOMAPP uses a hierarchical SKU system: SERIES-STYLE-SERIAL-MODIFIER
Example SKU: NAC-SE01-EHM

section.title: Pricing Model
section.p: ECOMAPP uses a multiplier-based pricing system where each product variant's final price is calculated by multiplying the base price by a modifier-specific multiplier.
The formula is Final Price = BASE_PRICE_CENTS x PRICE_MULTIPLIER.

p.note: Users who upload a new product will be able to decipher the price for their product. The pricing model does show the user a recommended price.

ul.li: TOKENS - Authentication tokens (placeholder for future implementation)
INVENTORY - Seller listings with pricing and stock levels
ORDER_ADDRESSES - Immutable address snapshots per order
ORDER_ITEMS - Line items in orders with pricing snapshots
Add CREATE UNIQUE INDEX for primary key
Add foreign key constraints if needed
Add performance indexes for new table
Add COMMENT statements for new tables and columns
Add GRANT statement for new table
Add INSERT statements for test data
Add DROP TABLE statement in appropriate position.

p.codeblock: 1. Update E03TABLE.sql with new table definition 2. Submit ECOMDROP 3. Submit ECOMDDL 4. Submit ECOMLOAD (reloads test data)

task.domains: (topic task) (topic abbrev-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (topic equation-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic mathml-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic svg-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic markup-d xml-d) (topic task strictTaskbody-c)
task.title: Installing ECOMAPP
task.shortdesc: ECOMAPP database is hosted on Db2 for z/OS platform. Installing ECOMAPP requires you to have a Db2 license called Db2 Connect in order for you to make a connection from the NXTCG backend application.

prereq.p: Before installing ECOMAPP, you must have these requirements:

ul.li: Db2: Version 13, subsystem ID <Your SSID>, Db2 software HLQ <Your DB2HLQ>, and a Db2 Connect license.
Storage: 100 tracks for DDL library, 50 tracks for JCL library, 500 MB DASD for tablespaces and indexes.

taskbody.context: Installing ECOMAPP database requires you to have a Db2 Connect license. Below are steps instructing you on how to install ECOMAPP database within TSO/ISPF.

step.cmd: Customize Placeholders. Before uploading any files, you must customize the following placeholders throughout all DDL and JCL files:

chrow.chdesc: Application user group for DML access

step.cmd: Create an Initial Partitioned Data Set (PDS) in <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALOC. You must create one dataset and upload the ECOMALOC.jcl as a member of that dataset. It can be accessed by <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALLOC(ECOMALOC).
step.info: Log into TSO/ISPF and allocate the Initial PDS:Command ===> A Dataset Name: <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ECOMALOC Management class . . . <blank> Storage class . . . . <blank> Volume serial . . . . <blank> Device type . . . . . <blank or SYSDA> Data class . . . . . . <blank> Space units . . . . . TRKS Primary quantity . . . 5 Secondary quantity . . 2 Directory blocks . . . 2 Record format . . . . FB Record length . . . . 80 Block size . . . . . . 3120 Data set name type . . PDSUpload ECOMALOC.jcl via Zowe or by pasting in the code in ISPF. You can find and edit it by using option 3.4 to find <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.ALLOC(ECOMALLOC)

cmd.b: Run ECOMALOC to create DDL and JCL libraries. This job creates the two main PDS libraries for the DDL and JCL files.

ol.li: Type SUB next to the ECOMALOC member

cmd.b: Upload all SQL DDL files to the <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL library.

ol.li:
For each DDL file, create a new member:
Option 2 in DSLIST from the ISPF main menu
Insert <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL(<membername>)
Paste in the new member contents You can use Zowe to upload members directly from the project repository into the dataset.

p.b: Upload these members in this order:

cmd.b: Upload all JCL job files to the <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.JCL library.

ol.li:
For each JCL file, create a new member:
Option 2 in DSLIST from the ISPF main menu
Insert <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL(<membername>)
Paste in the new member contents

cmd.b: Submit the ECOMDDL job to create the database.

ol.li: Insert B for browsing to see the members.
Type SUB next to the ECOMDDL member

note.p:
This job executes the complete DDL pipeline in dependency order:
Steps Description
STGDB Step Creates ECOMDB01 database
TBSPC Step Creates 15 tablespaces
TABLES Step Creates all tables with PKs, FKs, constraints
INDEXES Step Creates performance indexes
COMMENTS Step Adds catalog comments
GRANTS Step Grants privileges to owner and application user

step.info:
TSO/ISPF with Db2
Option ==> D (Db2I Primary Menu) Option ==> 2 (SPUFI)
Enter SQL: SELECT * FROM SYSIBM.SYSTABLES WHERE CREATOR = '<Your HLQ>';
You should see 15 tables: USERS, TOKENS, SERIES, STYLES, STYLE_MODIFIERS, PRODUCT_TYPES, PRODUCT_VARIANTS, VARIANT_IMAGES, INVENTORY, CARTS, CART_ITEMS, ADDRESSES, ORDERS, ORDER_ADDRESSES, ORDER_ITEMS

info.p: After creating the database structure, initialize it with the NXTCG catalog.

ol.li: Type SUB next to ECOMINIT member

note.p: Executes E07INIT1, E07INIT2, and E07INIT3 in sequence to populate the product catalog:

info.p: After creating the database, run statistics for optimal query performance:

ol.li: Type SUB next to ECOMRS member
Check the job output in SDSF job status menu

info.note: You must run ECOMRS before running ECOMRPTS.

topic.domains: (topic abbrev-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (topic equation-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic mathml-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic svg-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic markup-d xml-d)
topic.title: Troubleshooting the NXTCG ECOMAPP database
topic.shortdesc: There are common SQL and API error codes when configuring the ECOMAPP database. Solutions are provided for each issue.

title.b: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=12 - PLAN NOT FOUND

section.p: Cause: Db2 plan DSNTEP3 not available or no EXECUTE privilege

title.b: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=12 - SQLCODE=-204 OBJECT NOT FOUND

section.p: Cause: DDL members not uploaded or incorrect dataset names

p.codeblock:
1. Verify DDL members exist: TSO/ISPF 3.4 → <Your HLQ>.ECOMAPP.DDL
2. Check member names match exactly: E01STGDB, E02TBSPC, E03TABLE, E04INDEX, E05CMNT, E06GRANT E07INIT1, E07INIT2, E07INIT3
3. Verify SET HLQ statement in ECOMDDL.jcl matches your dataset name

title.b: ECOMDDL Fails with RC=8 - SQLCODE=-601 OBJECT ALREADY EXISTS

section.p: Cause: Database objects already exist (normal on re-run)

p.codeblock:
This is expected behavior when re-running ECOMDDL on existing database. Job will continue and create any missing objects.
To start fresh: 1. Submit ECOMDROP first 2. Then submit ECOMDDL

title.b: ECOMINIT Fails with RC=12 - Rest API failure with HTTP(S) status 500

section.p: Cause: SQL files have lines exceeding 72 characters

p.codeblock:
All E07INIT files in zOS_Datasets have been pre-processed to comply with 72-character line limits. If you modified them:
1. Check for long lines: Edit member with ISPF COLS ON (show column numbers) Look for lines extending past column 72
2. Split long lines: - Break INSERT statements after table name - Continue column list on next line with leading spaces - Ensure VALUES clause on separate line
3. Re-upload corrected members

title.b: ECOMINIT Fails with SQLCODE=-803 DUPLICATE KEY

section.p: Cause: Product catalog already loaded

p.codeblock:
This is expected behavior with RC=8. Job continues to next step. Each step is conditioned on RC < 12.
To reload catalog from scratch: 1. Submit ECOMDROP first 2. Submit ECOMDDL 3. Submit ECOMINIT

section.p: Cause: Previous data not cleared, or partial load

p.codeblock:
-- Check current countsSELECT'PRODUCT_VARIANTS'AS TABLE_NAME,COUNT(*)AS COUNT FROM<Your HLQ>.PRODUCT_VARIANTS;
Expected: 4,824If count doesn't match: 1. Submit ECOMDROP 2. Submit ECOMDDL 3. Submit ECOMINIT

section.p: Cause: Insufficient Db2 privileges

p.codeblock:
Contact your Db2 DBA to grant CREATEDB authority:
GRANT CREATEDB ON SYSTEM TO <Your HLQ>;

section.p: Cause: Incorrect Db2 software library HLQ
Cause: E01STGDB.sql uses default storage group

p.codeblock:
1. Find available storage groups: SELECT * FROM SYSIBM.SYSSTOGROUP;
2. Update E01STGDB.sql: CREATE DATABASE ECOMDB01 STOGROUP <Your STOGROUP> BUFFERPOOL BP0 ...

section.p: Cause: Query not joining to STYLE_MODIFIERS table

p.codeblock: -- Incorrect (missing PRICE_MULTIPLIER):SELECT PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS FROM PRODUCT_VARIANTS PV INNERJOIN PRODUCT_TYPES PT ON...;-- Correct (includes multiplier):SELECT PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS, SM.PRICE_MULTIPLIER, CAST(PT.BASE_PRICE_CENTS * SM.PRICE_MULTIPLIER ASINT)AS FINAL_PRICE FROM PRODUCT_VARIANTS PV INNERJOIN PRODUCT_TYPES PT ON...INNERJOIN STYLE_MODIFIERS SM ON PV.STYLE_CODE = SM.STYLE_CODE AND PV.MODIFIER_CODE = SM.MODIFIER_CODE;

section.p: Cause: Statistics not gathered or outdated

p.codeblock:
1. Submit ECOMRS to gather/refresh statistics 2. Verify statistics exist: SELECT NAME, CARD, NPAGES FROM SYSIBM.SYSTABLES WHERE CREATOR = '<Your HLQ>';
If CARD = -1, statistics not gathered
